label: Culiacán — Primary Battleground
sub: State capital and cartel civil war epicenter. Home to 1 million residents, the city has recorded 2,400+ conflict-related killings since September 2024, with firefights erupting near schools and hospitals.

label: La Campiña — Chapitos Stronghold
sub: East Culiacán neighborhood and key Chapitos base. Site of the September 9, 2024 opening clash when Mexican Army troops encountered armed civilians, killing a sergeant and triggering the civil war.

label: Tierra Blanca — Repeated Clashes
sub: Culiacán neighborhood and persistent flashpoint. Site of June 15–16, 2025 battles where La Mayiza used a .50-caliber rifle to destroy a Chapitos armored truck, killing at least 3.

label: Costa Rica — Narco-Messages
sub: Culiacán neighborhood where bodies with faction narco-messages were found January 6, 2025. La Mayiza-affiliated operatives claimed responsibility for killings of Chapitos gunmen.

label: Huertos del Pedregal — Mayo's Kidnapping Site
sub: Ranch on the outskirts of Culiacán where El Mayo Zambada was allegedly ambushed on July 25, 2024. Federal forensic evidence found blood and ballistic evidence matching El Mayo's account, contradicting the official state narrative.

label: Culiacán International Airport
sub: Key transport hub used by cartel leadership and under contested control. Military forces maintain a checkpoint presence. Airport flights disrupted multiple times during peak violence in late 2024.

label: Los Mochis — La Mayiza Northern HQ
sub: Major northern Sinaloa city and La Mayiza stronghold. Port of Topolobampo nearby provides maritime drug export options. La Mayiza has maintained firm control throughout the conflict.

label: Mazatlán — Tourist Hub Hit by Violence
sub: Sinaloa's largest Pacific coast tourist city. Recorded 95 homicides January–September 2025, a 227% increase over 2024. Both factions contest control of the city's lucrative criminal plaza.

label: Badiraguato — El Guano's Sierra Stronghold
sub: El Chapo's birthplace and Golden Triangle hub controlled by El Guano (La Mayiza). April 22, 2026: Major federal operation with 5 Black Hawk helicopters targeted El Guano here — multiple detainees confirmed, but El Guano evaded (SEDENA confirmed Apr 23). ~100 families displaced; population dropped nearly 30% since Sep 2024.

label: La Tuna — El Chapo's Birthplace
sub: Tiny village in Badiraguato municipality where Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán was born. Symbolic stronghold for the Chapitos faction. Heavily fortified during the conflict; civilian population has largely fled.

label: Bacacoragua — Drone Attack Site
sub: Community in Badiraguato municipality struck by armed drone attacks in September 2024. Electricity cut and roads blocked. Residents fled en masse, making it one of the first mountain communities forcibly abandoned in the conflict.

label: Guasave — Chapo Isidro Territory
sub: Northern Sinaloa municipality and stronghold of Fausto 'Chapo Isidro' Meza Flores and his Vitache Special Forces, who joined La Mayiza. Site of the December 2024 record fentanyl seizure of 20 million pills.

label: Ahome — Record Drug Bust
sub: Northern Sinaloa municipality adjacent to Los Mochis. Site of Mexico's largest-ever fentanyl seizure in December 2024 (20 million pills), conducted jointly with Guasave. Under La Mayiza's operational umbrella.

label: El Fuerte — Northern Chapitos Territory
sub: Far northern Sinaloa municipality reported under Los Chapitos control during the territorial split crystallized by late 2024. Historic colonial town serving as a strategic gateway to Sonora.

label: Mocorito — Chapitos-Controlled
sub: Central Sinaloa municipality reported under Los Chapitos' control during the territorial split. Lies between the Sierra Madre foothills and the coastal plain, making it a key logistical corridor.

label: Angostura — Chapitos Territory
sub: Municipality in north-central Sinaloa under Los Chapitos control. Angostura sits along the Sinaloa River agricultural plain and is strategically important for cartel logistics and revenue from local extortion.

label: Navolato — Gateway to Culiacán Bay
sub: Municipality directly west of Culiacán, reported under Los Chapitos control. Access to the Pacific Coast via Altata beach area. Historically important for maritime drug smuggling operations.

label: San Ignacio — La Mayiza Zone
sub: Southern Sinaloa municipality reported under La Mayiza control. Located in rugged sierra terrain, San Ignacio provides a buffer between Mazatlán and the mountain drug cultivation zones.

label: Elota — La Mayiza Southern Territory
sub: Central-southern Sinaloa municipality under La Mayiza control. Part of the zone securing the faction's access to southern coastal trafficking corridors toward Nayarit and Jalisco.

label: Concordia — Highest Displacement Count
sub: Southern Sinaloa municipality with the highest number of displaced families in the conflict: 261 families by mid-2025. Sites including El Palmito and El Espinal saw some of the war's worst rural fighting.

label: El Palmito — Evacuated Sept 2024
sub: Rural community in Concordia municipality. Approximately 180–200 residents evacuated in September 2024 and relocated to Mazatlán. One of the first mass civilian evacuations of the conflict.

label: El Espinal — Burned Vehicles Jan 2025
sub: Community in Concordia municipality where heavy La Chapiza vs La Mayiza fighting was documented January 4, 2025, with multiple burned-out vehicles photographed. Repeated clashes have effectively depopulated the area.

label: Cosalá — Multi-Faction Contested
sub: Central Sinaloa municipality with 238 displaced families recorded by mid-2025. Unique for reportedly having more than one faction controlling territory simultaneously, creating a patchwork of control that makes civilian life untenable.

label: Rosario — Southern Sinaloa
sub: Southernmost significant Sinaloa municipality. Displacement reported throughout the conflict. The municipality borders Nayarit state, making it strategically important for southward trafficking routes.

label: Surutato — La Mayiza Sierra Control
sub: Mountain community in the Sierra Madre Occidental reported taken by La Mayiza on May 19, 2025. The high-altitude region is critical for poppy cultivation, clandestine meth labs, and cartel leadership refuge.

label: Los Sitios — Sierra Cleared by Mayiza
sub: Remote Sierra Madre community whose control was seized by La Mayiza in May 2025 operations, part of a broader sweep of mountain territory previously held or contested by Los Chapitos.

label: Potrerillos del Norote — Body Display
sub: Town in central Sinaloa where a body was found hanging from arches on March 3, 2025, with a narco-message. The incident is part of a pattern of psychological warfare body displays occurring across the state.

label: Culiacán Landfill Displacement Camp
sub: Improvised settlement near the Culiacán municipal landfill where displaced families from rural Sinaloa established emergency camps. Serious shortages of food, water, and medical care documented by humanitarian organizations.

label: Durango — CJNG Incursion Front
sub: State capital of Durango and gateway for CJNG gunmen deployed to support Los Chapitos in the Sinaloa-Durango border zone. CJNG reportedly launched offensives from Durango against La Mayiza-affiliated factions in 2025.

label: Tamazula — Sinaloa-Durango Border Fighting
sub: Municipality on the Durango side of the Sierra Madre border with Sinaloa. Site of sustained fighting between CJNG-backed Chapitos and La Mayiza, as both factions contest the Golden Triangle cultivation zones.

label: Mazatlán Zona Dorada — Tourist Strip Hit
sub: Mazatlán's tourist Golden Zone has seen proximity fighting and armed encounters as cartel factions contest the lucrative criminal plaza. Multiple shooting incidents occurred within blocks of major hotel complexes in 2025.

label: Culiacán — Third Military Region HQ
sub: Mexican Army's Third Military Region headquarters in Culiacán, the operational command for Sinaloa. New commander General Briseño Lobera was installed October 2, 2024. The base coordinates up to 14,000 soldiers deployed across Sinaloa and adjacent states.

label: Highway 15 — Cartel Checkpoint Zone
sub: Mexico's Pacific Coast Highway 15 (Culiacán to Los Mochis) is subject to cartel checkpoints where armed groups verify identities and control movement. The highway is the main north-south economic artery of Sinaloa.

label: Hwy 15 (Mazatlán–Culiacán) — Contested
sub: The Mazatlán–Culiacán section of Highway 15 has seen multiple roadblocks, armed checkpoints, and ambushes by both factions. The corridor is vital for commerce but has become dangerous for civilian travelers.

label: Sierra Madre — Clandestine Meth Lab
sub: Remote Sierra Madre location where Mexican authorities seized a clandestine methamphetamine laboratory in 2025. The area's isolation makes it ideal for large-scale drug synthesis. Multiple labs have been discovered and destroyed in the mountain range.

label: Ahome — Fentanyl Processing Facility
sub: Northern Sinaloa industrial area where the December 2024 record seizure of 20 million fentanyl pills was made. Large-scale pill press and precursor chemical stockpile discovered alongside the pills.

label: Culiacán — Urban Fentanyl Lab Seizure
sub: Urban neighborhood lab discovered during security operations in Culiacán. Authorities found pill presses, precursor chemicals, and finished fentanyl product. Chapitos-faction labs have been identified in residential neighborhoods.

label: Guasave — Industrial Drug Lab
sub: Guasave municipality drug production facility seized by Mexican federal police. The area, controlled by Chapo Isidro's La Mayiza-aligned forces, has multiple known drug processing locations in agricultural outbuildings.

label: Topolobampo Port — Maritime Drug Export
sub: Deep-water Pacific port near Los Mochis used by La Mayiza for maritime drug export operations. Among the most important Pacific coast export points for Sinaloa Cartel product heading to Asian and South American markets.

label: Choix — Sinaloa-Chihuahua Border
sub: Far northern Sinaloa municipality on the border with Chihuahua. Los Chapitos have maintained influence in this remote border zone, which provides access to cartel operations in Chihuahua state.

label: Guamúchil — Regional Market City
sub: Salvador Alvarado municipality and commercial hub of central-northern Sinaloa. Los Chapitos have used the city's commercial economy for extortion revenue and money laundering operations.

label: Escuinapa — Southern Gateway
sub: Southernmost Sinaloa municipality near the Nayarit border. Under La Mayiza control and important for southward drug trafficking toward Guadalajara. Shrimp and agriculture economy has been extorted by cartel operations.

label: Mazatlán Port — Smuggling Hub
sub: Major Pacific port used for maritime drug exports and imports of precursor chemicals. Security forces maintain a presence but cartel infiltration of port operations has been documented.

label: Culiacán Federal Court — Prosecutorial Hub
sub: Federal court in Culiacán handling cartel prosecutions. Multiple local commanders and financial operators from both factions have been processed here. Security is extreme due to repeated threats against prosecutors and judges.

label: El Dorado — Agricultural Extortion Zone
sub: Agricultural community south of Culiacán where cartel factions extort tomato, chili, and vegetable growers. Repeated confrontations between La Mayiza and Chapitos-aligned groups have disrupted the agricultural economy.

label: Imala — Mountain Foothills Clash Zone
sub: Foothills community northeast of Culiacán where cartel control transitions from lowland Chapitos territories to highland contested zones. Persistent low-level fighting documented throughout 2025.

label: Culiacán — Rehab Center Massacre (Apr 2026)
sub: Drug rehabilitation center in Culiacán where 8 people were killed in early April 2026 by Chapitos gunmen searching for a La Mayiza rival. Consistent with a pattern of both factions targeting civilian infrastructure to hunt enemies.

label: Culiacán Naval Base — March 2026 Raid HQ
sub: Mexican Navy (SEMAR) base in Culiacán that coordinated the March 19, 2026 raid killing 11 and capturing plaza boss El Patas. Navy has taken an increasingly prominent role in urban countercartel operations.

label: Villa Unión — Previous Cartel Flashpoint
sub: Small town in southern Sinaloa near Rosario with a history of cartel violence. The conflict has extended into this area with both factions competing for control of trafficking corridors through the municipality.

label: Badiraguato Sierra — Chapitos Mountain Camp
sub: Remote highland camp in Badiraguato sierra used as a staging area and refuge by Los Chapitos fighters. The rugged terrain provides natural defenses and historically has been nearly impenetrable to security forces.

label: Culiacán Hospital Zone — Combat Overflow
sub: Hospital district in central Culiacán overwhelmed by conflict casualties throughout late 2024. Physicians Without Borders documented severe hospital capacity issues during peak violence periods. Armed incidents occurred near hospital entrances.

label: Tecualilla — Police Ambush (Mar 31, 2026)
sub: Site on the Mazatlán–Tepic highway near Tecualilla, Escuinapa municipality where a cartel hit squad ambushed municipal police on March 31, 2026, killing 4 officers including the operational subdirector of the Escuinapa police command. One officer critically injured. Occurred 3 days into Semana Santa security surge.

label: La Palma, Cosalá — Chapitos Meth Lab (Apr 3, 2026)
sub: Clandestine methamphetamine laboratory in La Palma, Cosalá municipality dismantled by Mexican Navy (SEMAR) on April 3, 2026. Chapitos-linked. Seized: 1,000 liters of phenylacetone (P2P), 44 liters of toluene, 100 kg caustic soda. No armed confrontation during raid.

label: Sonora Border — Fentanyl Northbound Route
sub: Sinaloa-Sonora border crossing zone used for northbound fentanyl trafficking toward the US. Disrupted independent producers relocated from Culiacán to this area after the Chapitos imposed licensing requirements on fentanyl production.

label: Culiacán — El Patas Capture Site (Mar 19, 2026)
sub: Location in Culiacán where Mexican Navy forces captured Omar 'El Patas' Torres, La Mayiza's plaza boss for this sector, on March 19, 2026. Eleven people killed in the raid. Torres's daughter was released at the scene.

label: La Costerita — Good Friday Police Killing (Apr 4, 2026)
sub: Punta Azul sector on the Benito Juárez bypass ('La Costerita'), where municipal officer Jose Luis was shot multiple times while riding his motorcycle on April 4, 2026 — the 34th Culiacán officer killed since September 2024. Eight total murders in Sinaloa on Good Friday; state led the nation with 7 intentional homicides.

label: Escuinapa — 5th Officer Killed at Home (Apr 8, 2026)
sub: An Escuinapa municipal police officer was intercepted and killed at his home on April 8, 2026 — the 5th officer killed in 9 days since the March 31 highway ambush. 32 officers (~40% of the municipal force) subsequently resigned. Governor Rocha Moya deployed 1,190 troops in response. The home killing eliminated any sense of off-duty safety, triggering the mass resignation.

label: Culiacán — Boulevard Madero Drive-By (Apr 10, 2026)
sub: Two men killed in a drive-by shooting at Boulevard Francisco I. Madero at Venustiano Carranza Ave, Culiacán, at 5:30 PM on April 10, 2026. Victims were in a white Mazda; one identified as Christian Manuel (30, nurse). A 62-year-old female bus passenger injured by shrapnel. Part of ongoing Chapitos–Mayitos urban violence pattern.

label: Mazatlán — Chapitos Arrests (Apr 19, 2026)
sub: Two Los Chapitos faction members arrested with drugs and assault rifles in Mazatlán on April 19, 2026. Part of ongoing security force interdiction operations in the contested tourist city, where homicides rose 227% since the Sinaloa civil war began.

label: Escuinapa–Teacapán Hwy — 5 Chapitos Arrested (Apr 16, 2026)
sub: A joint federal operation (SSPC, SEDENA, FGR, National Guard) with aerial support detained five alleged Chapitos members on the Escuinapa–Teacapán highway on April 16, 2026. Seized: 7 long-barrel firearms, 1 pistol, 6 ballistic vests, 1 vehicle. Southern Sinaloa's contested corridor toward Nayarit state. Combined with the April 19 Mazatlán arrests, 7 Chapitos detained in one week.

label: Sierra Tarahumara — CIA Drug Lab Raid / Crash Site (Apr 21, 2026)
sub: Site in Morelos municipality, Chihuahua (Sierra Tarahumara / Golden Triangle) where a joint CIA–Chihuahua AEI operation destroyed six clandestine drug labs on April 20–21, 2026. Returning officers — 2 CIA and 2 Mexican AEI — died in a vehicle crash on the mountain road. Described by authorities as 'one of the largest drug production sites found in the country.' President Sheinbaum demanded investigation after learning the federal government had not been notified of the US intelligence operation.

label: Tamazula — 10 Gente del Guano Arrested (Apr 23, 2026)
sub: Tamazula, on the Sinaloa-Durango border within the Golden Triangle, where SEDENA/SEMAR forces (with US intelligence support) arrested 10 Gente del Guano members on April 23, 2026 — including El Guano's right-hand man 'Abel N', his chief bodyguard, and his financial/logistics operator. Weapons, explosives seized. El Guano evaded for the fourth time. Described by El Financiero as 'the second attempt to capture El Guano.' His $5M US State Dept reward remains outstanding.

label: Ejido Balbuena, Navolato — Armed Ambush (Apr 26, 2026)
sub: Site of an armed ambush in the early morning of April 26, 2026 that killed one unidentified male (~40 yrs). At least two vehicles of gunmen intercepted the victim meters from his home; ~100 rifle shell casings recovered. Adjacent municipality to Culiacán; contested for coastal Altata corridor access. Part of ongoing Chapitos–La Mayiza violence pattern in the Navolato area.

label: Durango City — 'El Culiacancito' Displacement Zone
sub: Southeastern Durango City neighborhoods — particularly Villas Campestre, Privanzas, and Los Laureles — where displaced Culiacán families have concentrated since late 2024, earning the area the nickname 'El Culiacancito.' Rents rose 50%+ over 12 months. School enrollment from Culiacán surged from September 2025. A real estate agent and valuators documented the trend; Revista Espejo and N+ reported on it April 25, 2026. Represents the geographic reach of the Sinaloa civil war's displacement crisis beyond state borders.
